Employee Log in as employee Candidate Log in to Connect Homepage paradoxinteractive.com
Design · Stockholm - Studio Black
**Game Designer Crusader Kings III**
====================================


Apply for this job


As a Crusader Kings III Game Designer, you will work as a generalist developer: designing, implementing, assessing, and refining new features in a live game with a passionate fanbase. You will translate historical research into dynamic systems, perform narrative design and writing, implement features in script, balance game mechanics, and fix bugs. You not only come up with great ideas but take point in bringing them to life, working alongside programmers, artists, UX designers, and QA analysts to make sure the features live up to player expectations.


One week in the life of a CK3 Game Designer can look wildly different from the next. You might be crafting one-off narrative events or critical player interactions, balancing opinion modifiers or the economy, researching and scripting hundreds of dynastic lineages and titles, or contributing to design discussions or critical feedback sessions. Our work simultaneously demands a great deal of ownership while being highly collaborative.


Our tools are flexible and powerful, but complex. You should be comfortable and self-directed in a technical development environment, being able to troubleshoot issues and getting yourself unblocked. Experience with programming or scripting is a strong plus.


Our games are renowned as uniquely complex in the industry. That complexity is made possible by the unique skillsets of the people who craft them. We set high standards on ourselves while helping those around us grow as developers, regardless of whether you're a well-established professional or at the beginning of your journey.

**Example of responsibilities**

* Design, prototype, and implement gameplay features, mechanics, and narrative content for Crusader Kings III.
* Research medieval history and cultural practices to inspire new mechanics and content.
* Participate in design discussions, feature concepting, and design bashes with project leadership.
* Review and provide feedback on the work of other designers through merge reviews and peer discussions.
* Iterate on implemented features based on testing, feedback, and team discussions.

**The skills we’re searching for…**

* Experience with game design and gameplay implementation.
* Comfort working as a generalist designer across systems, content, and narrative.
* Experience in implementing designs in script or code.
* Curious and solution-oriented mindset.
* Strong communication and collaboration skills.
* Fluency in English.

**We consider it a plus if you have…**

* Experience modding or scripting Paradox titles.
* Strong familiarity with Crusader Kings III.
* A deep interest in strategy / management / simulation games with complex systemic design.


Paradox is a nerdy place, and we tend to attract nerdy people. We value developers who are genuinely passionate about something, whether that is games, history rabbit holes, fiction writing, philosophy, cooking, mathematics, or music production. If you enjoy complex strategy games or simulations and thinking about how systems interact, you will feel at home with us. While expertise in Paradox games is valuable, enthusiasm for any games with deep and intricate systems - Total War, Mount & Blade, Anno, Caves of Qud, Kenshi, or similar - is just as relevant.


If this sounds like you, we would love to hear from you!


Practical information  

Scope: Full time and permanent  

Reports to: Managing Design Lead  

Location: Paradox Development Studio Black, Stockholm


The position is full time and on-site.

  


Department
Design
Role
Game Designer
Locations
Stockholm - Studio Black

title: Configurator and Technical Expert
description:
***Key*** **points**
--------------------


* Cloud Applications
* Spend Management
* Databases
* System integration
**Summary**
-----------



In relation with solution consultants and project managers, you are in charge of the **implementation** and maintenance of **cloud applications** such as Ivalua and GEP, and have the responsibility of on-time delivery, quality controls, and 2nd level support for **strategic projects**. You take part in the design and architecture of data interfaces between these applications and other systems such as ERPs, accounting systems, and online ordering platforms.



**OJC Consulting - Malmö, Sweden**
----------------------------------



Based in Sweden since 2018, OJC Consulting has been supporting major Swedish and Norwegian companies in their strategic digital transformation projects by providing them consulting and implementation services for corporate cloud solutions. Our business consultants and web applications specialists, based in Stockholm and Malmö, help international companies increase their profitability by improving their business processes, optimizing costs and leveraging cutting-edge software solutions.



**Duties and responsibilities**
-------------------------------


* Functional and technical design: participate in design workshops, design user and system interfaces on Ivalua or GEP platforms; contribute to functional and technical specifications.
* End to end implementation, testing and delivery of the designed solution. Most of the implementation is done through online configuration of the cloud solutions, and requires advanced SQL skills.
* Manage the maintenance cycle of projects in production, including 2nd level support, problem identification and resolution.
**Qualification and Experience**
--------------------------------


* Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science or Information Technology.
* Proven experience in design, architecture and development or web applications in the context of large corporations.
* Understanding of Project Management processes, customer relationships and business analysis.
* Excellent communication and analytical skills.
* Strong technical skills, esp. on SQL and system interfaces
**Skills**
----------


* Web application design (architecture, UX/UI)
* Databases (SQL Server), BI, ETL
* Project methodology
* Strong analytical and reporting skills
* Detail oriented, autonomous, deeply involved
* Well-structured expression and writing
**Benefits**
------------


* Flexibility to work from home or in an office environment
* Great progress opportunities
* International, fast growing company
* Enjoy great autonomy and variety of tasks
* Many benefits

title: UX/UI Designer with Frontend Development Skills
description:
At Evolution AB / Nolimit City, we create world-class gaming products with bold, uncompromising design and unmatched attention to detail. We're looking for a UX/UI Designer who doesn't just stop at the design phase — you also bring your ideas to life in code.
 



  





 You'll join a cross-functional product team with full ownership over the solutions you design, working on innovative and highly interactive user experiences seen by players around the world. You’ll collaborate closely with engineers to bring your ideas to life—creating products that are both beautiful and usable. Depending on your experience, you might implement parts of the frontend yourself or work alongside developers to ensure your vision is realized in the final product.
 



  





**What You'll Do** 



* Lead UX and UI design efforts for new and existing products — from user flows and wireframes to high-fidelity mockups.
* Translate your designs into functional, responsive, and accessible frontend components using React (Next.js) and TypeScript.
* Partner with developers and stakeholders to iterate on solutions, run experiments, and refine interfaces based on user feedback and performance data.
* Build and maintain components in a shared design system, ensuring consistency and scalability across products.
* Advocate for user-centered design throughout the product development process.



  





**You Might Be a Great Fit If You** 



* Have a strong background in UX/UI design, with a portfolio showcasing thoughtful, elegant interfaces and interaction design.
* Are fluent in modern design tools like Figma, and enjoy prototyping and testing user flows.
* Know how to write clean, maintainable frontend code using React and TypeScript.
* Are comfortable working with developers and understand how design decisions impact performance and implementation.
* Care deeply about usability, accessibility, and the overall user experience.
* Enjoy working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment where experimentation is encouraged.



  





**Bonus Points** 



* Experience with Next.js, Redux Toolkit, or Headless CMSs like Strapi.
* Familiarity with A/B testing, analytics, and data-driven design decisions.
* An understanding of game or entertainment product design patterns.



  





**Technologies You'll Touch** 



* Figma (or other tools you prefer) / Design Systems
* React / Next.js
* TypeScript / JavaScript
* Redux Toolkit
* CSS / Tailwind CSS
* Strapi (Headless CMS)
* Git (GitHub Actions)



  





**Who we are** 




 You don't need to know everything from day one. If you're a designer who loves to get your hands dirty in code, and you're passionate about crafting exceptional user experiences, we'd love to meet you.
 



  





 Join one of Sweden's fastest-growing companies and help shape the future of interactive entertainment.
 



  





 Nolimit City, Stockholm based development team. We provide you with your choice of OS and IDE.
 



 Apply on LinkedIn, or send your application to hr-sweden@nolimitcity.com
 



 http://www.nolimitcity.com

title: Product Designer
description:
**About Mynt** 




 Mynt is an all-in-one corporate card and spend management platform on a mission to reinvent business banking. By combining smart software with cards and payments infrastructure, we remove manual and time-consuming admin from how companies spend.
 



 We’re growing fast across the Nordics and have secured significant funding to support our continued expansion in Sweden and internationally.
 



 Mynt is a tech-first, product-driven company where teams are empowered to solve real problems and build things that matter. We’re a licensed e-money institution, card issuer, and payment processor, operating under the supervision of the Swedish Financial Supervisory Authority. Through strong partnerships with leading players such as Fortnox and Nordea — and many more on the way — our solutions are now live across the Nordics, Baltics, and Netherlands.
 



 Joining Mynt means helping shape both a category-defining product and a company that’s still early in its journey. With 100+ colleagues (and growing), we’re building teams that value ownership, momentum, and customer impact.
 



**About the role** 




**Design the experiences that shape our product and delight our users.** 




 At Mynt, we’re redefining how companies manage spending and payments. As a fast-growing fintech scale-up, our product and our people are at the core of everything we do. We’re now looking for a Product Designer to help craft intuitive, impactful experiences across our platform.
 



 You’ll take our product to the next level, using your creativity, judgment, and user-centric mindset to solve complex problems and elevate the user experience.
 



 As a
 **Product Designer** 
 at Mynt, you’ll work across the entire product lifecycle, from research and ideation to prototyping and final design. You’ll collaborate closely with engineers, product managers, and other designers to ensure every interaction is clear, delightful, and aligned with business goals. You’ll help shape design standards, systems, and processes that scale as our product and organization grow.
 



 You’ll also play a key role in advocating for users, validating ideas with data and testing, and continuously iterating to improve usability, accessibility, and satisfaction. At Mynt, design isn’t just about visuals, it’s about solving real problems for real people, at scale.
 



**What you bring to the table** 




 You have experience in product design and a proven ability to create user-centered experiences that deliver business impact. You thrive in collaborative, fast-moving environments and bring a thoughtful, data-informed approach to your work.
 



**You also bring:** 



* Experience in product or digital design
* Strong portfolio demonstrating end-to-end product design, including UX, UI, and interaction design
* Experience working closely with cross-functional teams (PM, engineering, research)
* Expertise in design tools (Figma, Sketch, or equivalent) and prototyping methods
* User-centered design mindset, with experience conducting research, usability testing, and synthesizing insights into actionable solutions
* Ability to advocate for users and translate feedback into design improvements
* Comfort with ambiguity, iteration, and shaping new processes and standards



**Why you’ll love it here** 



* Be part of a talented, passionate team in a fast-growing scale-up
* Collaborate with engineers and product managers to shape the future of our product
* Work with a modern, clean, and well-structured code and design system
* Influence both product direction and design culture at Mynt
* Enjoy a vibrant office in central Stockholm
* Shape your own development and responsibilities based on your interests and passion



*The position will be filled as soon as we find the right candidate. Reach out to us if you’re excited to help design the future of Mynt! 🌟*

title: UX Designer
description:
*Kreditz is transforming how businesses assess credit and risk using real-time data and NextGen Credit Scoring solutions.*
*As one of Europe’s fastest-growing FinTech scaleups - we’ve earned a place on the* 
***Sifted 100: Nordics & Benelux***
*list in both 2024 and 2025 and most recently, we were proud to receive a* 
***double win at the Credit Management & Compliance Awards 2025***
*, being named both "* 
***Fintech Rocket of the Year***
*" and "* 
***Breakthrough of the Year***
*".*
*With strong growth across the Nordics and Europe, our clients range from leading banks and iGaming giants to fast-moving digital innovators.*
**About The Position**
 We are looking for a
 **Senior UX Designer** 
 who can make complexity feel effortless.
   

  

 This is not a role for someone who only wants to “make things look pretty.” It’s a role for a strategic designer who thrives in high-stakes, data-heavy environments and who can turn sophisticated financial workflows into intuitive, world-class experiences.
   

  

**What Can Kreditz Offer**
* A once-in-a-lifetime growth journey, from startup to Global scaleup
* A journey where everyone in the team plays a crucial role for our company’s success
* Fun-loving, driven, and passionate colleagues who support each other’s success
* A culture that celebrates progress, humility, and teamwork
* We win together
* 24/7 access to gym, sauna and sports facilities at our office, plus additional wellness allowance
* Competitive base salary


**RESPONSIBILITES**
**You Will**
* Drive end-to-end user research to uncover deep behavioral patterns, maintaining a customer-centric mindset that bridges the gap between complex financial data and intuitive user experiences.
* Master the art of data-storytelling, translating quantitative findings into intuitive illustrations that make complex financial decisions feel like second nature.
* Translate complex technical and business requirements into clear problem definitions and seamless user journeys.
* Design high-velocity workflows where precision, clarity, and trust are non-negotiable.
* Create prototypes and iterate quickly in close collaboration with Product and Engineering.
* Facilitate workshops and design sprints with cross-functional teams to align stakeholders and accelerate delivery.
* Drive user-obsessed workflows and help establish UX as a scalable capability across multiple product domains.
* Support and evolve design patterns and system thinking across the platform.


 Success means users experience our platform as intuitive, confident, and effortless even when the underlying financial logic is complex.
   

  

**WE ARE LOOKING FOR**
 We believe that you are a strategic UX Designer with strong product instincts, high standards, and a deep interest in solving complex problems.
   

  

**You Likely**
* Have 5+ years of experience as a UX Designer, ideally within B2B SaaS and/or FinTech.
* Are comfortable working with financial data and using quantitative insights to validate decisions.
* Think in systems, not screens and care about scalability across products.
* Are strong in turning ambiguity into structure: clear problems, clear journeys, clear solutions.
* Collaborate naturally with developers and understand what it takes to implement design systems in real products.
* Communicate with clarity and presence — whether presenting to leadership or working hands-on with peers.
* Thrive in a fast-paced environment and can manage multiple initiatives without losing quality.


**Bonus Points If You Also Have**
* Strong qualitative research skills.
* Experience in high-transaction or high-risk product environments.
* Experience building or scaling design systems.


**WHY JOIN NOW?**
 At Kreditz, your work shapes how financial decisions are made across Europe. You’ll tackle real complexity, have real ownership, and help build a category-defining FinTech platform. With continued growth, and profitability on the horizon, strong investor backing, and a growing client base across Europe, we are entering our most exciting phase yet.
   

  

**APPOINTMENT OF ROLE**
 Q2 2026
   

  

**SCOPE**
 Full-time, permanent employment (tillsvidareanställning)
   

  

**SALARY**
 Competitive salary
   

  

**REPORTING**
 You will be reporting to the company’s CPO
   

  

**WORKPLACE**
 Danderyd, Stockholm
   

  

**RECRUITMENT PROCESS**
 Ongoing selection, we review candidates continuously.
   

  

*Last day for applications 31st March 2026*
**CRIMINAL RECORDS EXTRACT**
 As part of our recruitment process we will ask you to present a clean criminal records extract
   

  

**CONTACT**
 Lorenzo Puccio, VP Strategic Operations, Lorenzo@kreditz.com
   

  

 For more information about the role feel free to reach out to our CPO, Spilios Tzouras, spilios.tzouras@kreditz.com
   

  

 Intelligent credit- & risk decisions made easy with open banking & AI-technology

title: Product Designer
description:
**About Magma Math** 




 Magma is one of the world’s fastest-growing and most celebrated edtech companies, transforming math education through the power of AI. Our platform turns handwritten solutions into digital insights, empowering teachers to focus on creativity and deeper learning — while giving them real-time visibility into student progress.
 



  





 The result? More inclusive, engaging, and effective math classrooms for all learners.
 



  





 But we’re not just building better tools. We’re tackling one of the biggest challenges in global education: math underperformance. With 1 in 5 students globally struggling in math — a subject that forms the backbone of so many career paths — we believe the opportunity to improve outcomes at scale is massive.
 



  





 We’re already the go-to solution in thousands of schools — and we’re just getting started. As we expand rapidly across the U.S. and Europe, we’re building a team as ambitious as our mission: to help millions of students unlock their full potential in math.
 



  





**The Role** 




 We're looking for a
 **Product Designer** 
 to join our product team in Stockholm.
 



  





 In this role, your designs will quickly move from concept to reality. You’ll sketch, prototype, and refine solutions that are shipped into classrooms and districts across the world, where they’re put to use right away. That means you’ll get to see the impact of your work almost immediately - and improve it through real feedback.
 



  





 You’ll collaborate with product managers, engineers, and users to make sure every design is both functional and valuable.
 



  





 In this role, you’ll focus on our student platform, designing new experiences that boost engagement and help students feel excited — even proud — to do math.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* Create user flows, wireframes and hi-fi designs in Figma.
* Translate defined problems and solutions into clear, user-friendly designs.
* Collaborate closely with product managers, designers and engineers.
* Engage with users — from toddlers to high schoolers — to ground designs in real needs and deliver age-inclusive features.
* Focus on boosting the student engagement in the classroom that build loyalty and makes math (more) fun.



  





**Who You Are** 



* Background in product design (academic or equivalent work experience).
* Someone who enjoys solving problems and finding practical solutions.
* Curious and eager to learn, with a growth mindset.
* Always exploring better ways of working — new tools, new methods, and faster ways to prototype and learn.
* Comfortable working in a fast-paced, iterative environment, where features are released regularly and improved over time.
* Enjoys making sure every design is pixel perfect.
* Experience working with Design Systems.



  





**What We Offer** 



* Regular visits to schools to see the product in action, and to perform user tests.
* A role in a small but growing product team where your work will have visible impact.
* Fantastic colleagues in a fun environment with great opportunities to learn.
* Competitive salary and benefits including pension, insurance, and wellness perks.
* Annual company trip.
* An opportunity to work on a meaningful mission and have an exceptional impact on education, society, and how children learn math across the world.



  





**Start** 
 : As soon as possible
 



**Office location** 
 : Stockholm, Sweden
 



**Our Recruitment Process (all In Office)** 




 1st interview with Hiring Manager and Founder
 



 2nd interview with Hiring Manager and Magma member
 



 3rd interview with Team lead and Magma member

title: UI / Visual Designer
description:
At Extenda Retail, we build the systems that power the retail world—from high-traffic point-of-sale and self-checkout systems to complex inventory and back-office solutions. We are looking for a UI Designer who sets a high standard for visual excellence, polish, and consistency across complex enterprise products.
 



  





 You will join our UX team as a guardian of visual quality. This role is ideal for a designer who deeply cares about craft, but also understands how to design for scale, technical constraints, and long-term maintainability. You’ll be a key driver for the user interface layer across our product suite, defining visual standards, evolving our design system, and collaborating closely with UX, Engineering, and Product Management to deliver experiences that feel trustworthy, modern, and intuitive to both enterprise and consumer users.
 



  





 While you will partner closely with UX Designers, your center of gravity is UI excellence: layout, hierarchy, interaction patterns, design systems, accessibility, and that final layer of polish that separates “usable” from “exceptional.”
 



  





**This is initially a 12-month position, with a possibility to make the role permanent where you will join our UX team and play a vital role in advancing the visual quality of our product suite.** 




  





**Key Responsibilities** 




  




* **Design for Complexity:** 
 Create refined, scalable UI patterns for complex workflows across desktop, mobile, and specialized touch-screen environments.
* **System Evolution:** 
 Actively contribute to and evolve our design system—refining components, tokens, and visual standards to ensure a unified product language.
* **Cross-Functional Collaboration:** 
 Partner with UX Designers to translate research and wireframes into elegant interfaces, and work alongside Engineering to ensure pixel-perfect implementation.
* **Quality Assurance:** 
 Ensure visual consistency and accessibility are maintained across various product lines and release cycles.
* **Strategic Aesthetics:** 
 Balancing aesthetics with usability, accessibility, and technical feasibility.



  





**What You’ll Bring** 




  




* **Professional Experience:** 
 3–5 years of experience in UI Design, ideally within enterprise, SaaS, or complex software environments.
* **A Sharp Eye:** 
 Strong skills in typography, color theory, grid systems, and spacing.
* **Systems Thinking:** 
 You don’t just design screens; you design systems. You understand how a change in one component ripples across an entire ecosystem.
* **Communication:** 
 The ability to articulate the "why" behind your design decisions to stakeholders and developers alike.
* **Evidence of impact:** 
 A portfolio that showcases real-world products, demonstrating how you’ve navigated technical constraints without sacrificing quality.



  





**Required Skills & Experience** 




  




* **Design tools:** 
 Proficiency in Figma, including advanced use of auto-layout, components, and variables.
* **Accessibility First:** 
 Strong understanding of WCAG and EAA guidelines and how to implement them in design.
* **Agile Proficiency:** 
 Experience working within lean, iterative, and cross-functional product teams.
* **UX Foundations:** 
 A solid grasp of UX foundations to ensure your UI designs are backed by logic and that you can effectively collaborate with UX designers.



  





**Bonus Points** 




  




* **Workflow Innovation:** 
 Experience using AI design tools to accelerate your workflow and exploration.
* **Domain Expertise:** 
 Prior experience with POS, checkout, or complex enterprise systems.
* **Collaborative Spirit:** 
 You appreciate feedback, iteration, and getting eyes on your work early and often.

title: Product Designer - Growth
description:
**About Us**
 Soundtrack is a B2B scale-up company providing music streaming services for businesses. We serve small customers like the café around the corner, and much bigger brands like Joe & the Juice, Toni & Guy and TAG Heuer. On the inside, we're a bunch of talented, motivated and humble designers, engineers and music experts. We believe in product-led growth, where the product is the primary driver of customer acquisition, conversion and expansion.
   

  

**The Role**
 We're looking for a Product Designer for the Growth team at Soundtrack.
   

  

 The Growth team focuses on how Soundtrack is presented, discovered, and experienced for new users. This role strengthens our UI design capability and will play a central part in evolving our signup flows and onboarding experience.
   

  

 You’ll work closely with other Product Designers, Frontend Developers, Product Managers, Copywriters and Content creators in a highly collaborative, cross-functional environment. Design is a core part of problem-solving at Soundtrack, and you’ll be involved early — from uncovering user problems and sketching ideas to prototyping, iterating, and launching.
   

  

 In your day-to-day work, you’ll design onboarding experiences, create everything from intuitive UI to detailed components and visual assets. Prototype and iterate in Figma, and continuously refine solutions based on feedback and testing. You’re comfortable sharing work in progress, inviting input, and shaping ideas together with others.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Design and evolve onboarding and activation experiences that drive understanding, engagement, and conversion
* Create high-performing UI aligned with brand and design system
* Translate user and business problems into effective design solutions
* Prototype and iterate quickly based on feedback and testing
* Collaborate closely with developers to ensure high-quality implementation
* Contribute ideas and take ownership of design initiatives
* Validate solutions through user testing and experimentation when possible


**About You**
 Just like us, you love music. You're driven and entrepreneurial, but you know how to be a team player too. Regardless of roles, we're always looking to work with people who can adapt to constant change, prioritise what's important, stay humble, open, curious and with a passion for details.
   

  

 You’re a hands-on designer who enjoys producing and shipping. You’re passionate about creating highly functional design but also care about visual quality. You’re collaborative, open to feedback, and comfortable working iteratively.
   

  

**Requirements**
* 3+ years of experience as a UI Designer, Product Designer or similar
* Strong UI design skills together with solid visual and web design
* Proven experience designing signup, onboarding and activation journeys across web mobile apps and desktop apps
* Experience from a commercial digital environment (SaaS, product company, startup/scale-up, or agency)
* Proficiency in Figma
* Experience working within brand guidelines and design systems
* Strong collaboration skills across design, development, product, and marketing
* Portfolio showcasing digital user flows, product design and visual design


**Beneficial Requirements**
* Experience with conversion rate optimization (CRO) or data-informed design
* Experience conducting user testing or basic user research
* Experience working with experiments or A/B testing


**Employee Benefits**
* We offer a flexible workplace with the possibility to work remotely
* Generous pension plan
* Yearly budget for health, wellness and personal development
* Laptop and mobile phone of your choice with included cellular subscription
* Regular social activities such as team events, meet ups, after works and off-sites
* Daily breakfast served at the office


 At Soundtrack, we believe that diversity fuels creativity and innovation. We're committed to building a team that reflects a variety of backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences. We welcome applications from individuals of all races, ethnicities, genders, ages, abilities, religions, sexual orientations, and identities.
   

  

 We reserve the right to close this vacancy early if we identify a suitable candidate before the application deadline. To ensure consideration, we encourage you to submit your application as soon as possible.
   

  

 Please note that we only accept applications submitted via our career page and do not accept applications by e-mail.

title: Frontend Developer with focus on UX/UI
description:
At Evolution AB / Nolimit City, we're building cutting-edge gaming products that redefine how players experience online entertainment. As we continue to grow, we're looking for a Frontend Developer with a strong UX/UI focus, or a UX/UI Designer with frontend development chops, to join one of our expanding engineering teams.
 



  





 You'll play a key role in shaping the look, feel, and interactivity of products that reach a global audience—contributing not only to how things work but also how they feel. At Evolution, design and development are not siloed; you'll be an essential part of product decisions from day one, working in highly collaborative, autonomous teams.
 



  





**What You'll Do** 



* Design and build intuitive, responsive, and beautiful interfaces with React (Next.js) and TypeScript.
* Bring UX and UI designs to life—from wireframes to pixel-perfect implementation.
* Collaborate closely with product owners and engineers to craft seamless user experiences.
* Own the frontend from concept to production, including performance optimization, accessibility, and maintainability.
* Contribute to our design system and help shape a consistent visual language across products.
* Continuously experiment, iterate, and improve based on real user feedback and data.
* Be part of a team that values learning through iteration, experimentation, and shared responsibility.



  





**You Might Be a Great Fit If You** 



* Have solid experience with React, TypeScript, and modern CSS techniques.
* Possess a keen eye for design details, and care deeply about creating delightful user experiences.
* Enjoy bridging the gap between design and development.
* Are comfortable building advanced components.
* Believe in clean, maintainable code and smooth user flows.
* Have good communication skills in English and love working in a collaborative environment.



  





**Bonus Points** 



* Experience with Next.js, Redux Toolkit, or Headless CMSs like Strapi.
* Understanding of accessibility and web performance best practices.
* Familiarity with UI/UX testing tools or user research methods.
* An interest in gaming or digital entertainment interfaces.



  





**Technologies You'll Touch** 



* React / Next.js
* TypeScript / JavaScript
* Redux Toolkit
* CSS / Tailwind CSS
* Strapi (Headless CMS)
* Git (GitHub Actions)



  





**Who we are** 




 We care more about your potential than checking every box. If you're excited about building exceptional interfaces and love working in small, empowered teams, we want to hear from you.
 



  





 Join one of Sweden's fastest-growing tech companies and help us craft experiences that players around the world love.
 



  





 Nolimit City, Stockholm based development team. We provide you with your choice of OS and IDE.
 



  





 Apply on LinkedIn, or send your application to hr-sweden@nolimitcity.com
 



  





 http://www.nolimitcity.com

title: Design Director (visual/digital)
description:
Above is looking for a Design Director to join our creative team based in our Stockholm studio. This is a permanent full-time role.
   

  

**Job Description**
 As a Visual Design Director you will be a key player in driving cutting-edge design and innovation projects for some of the world’s most exciting brands. You will help us identify and secure new commercial opportunities, acting as a strategic partner for new and existing clients.
   

  

 With a high level of conceptual thinking, you will have a direct impact in shaping excellent project deliveries through craft and creativity, while also shaping our culture, team capabilities, and ways of working across disciplines.
   

  

 Our work is exciting and diverse in nature, spanning digital experiences such as websites, apps, and HMI. We collaborate across disciplines to create impact across all organizational levels and future horizons — from category-defining hero products to multidimensional digital ecosystems.
   

  

**What We Are Looking For**
 We are looking for an inspiring, forward-thinking Design Director who demonstrates excellence in craft, creativity, leadership, and emerging technologies:
   

  

* Forward-thinking approach to digital product experiences, with a keen interest in emerging trends and future developments
* Holistic thinker with strong conceptualization skills and critical-thinking abilities, comfortable leading conversations with stakeholders across design, technology, and business
* Demonstrated commercial experience in selling impactful digital experience projects — ideally supported by a strong professional network
* Strong storytelling and client leadership, with the ability to clearly communicate ideas and thought processes to both peers and clients
* Preferably 10+ years of professional experience, including familiarity with fast-paced agency environments and workflows
* Ability to lead, mentor, and grow multidisciplinary design teams, fostering creative excellence, psychological safety, and high performance
* Ability to lead clients through complex challenges and structure design processes that enable diverse teams to succeed
* Aesthetic sensibility in harmony with a thoughtful, user-centered approach to design, business, and technology
* Experience using — or actively driving — AI-enabled design workflows to explore ideas faster, prototype more intelligently, and elevate creative and strategic outcomes
* Ability to translate emerging AI capabilities into meaningful design direction, team practices, and client value
* A humble, self-propelled, and collaborative team player who feels at home in multi-competence project constellations


**A Bit About Above**
 We are a Scandinavian innovation agency dedicated to driving positive change through the integration of design, strategy, and technology. Our creative 360-approach allows us to break down boundaries between the physical and digital, while fusing hardware and software into meaningful experiences.
   

  

 We work with leading global brands, local Scandinavian heroes, and a fantastic collection of startups. We’re a team of warm, welcoming, dedicated professionals with diverse international backgrounds. In everything we do, we strive for world-class results and believe you share that mindset.
   

  

 Join a team that values collaboration, creativity, and tangible impact through thoughtful design.
   

  

**Join our team**
 Please submit your portfolio, CV, and personal letter.
   

  

 We welcome international applications and are generally able to assist with work permits and relocation.

title: UI/UX Design Manager - AI Powered (Relocation to Dubai)
description:
**Position:** 
 AI-First UI/UX Design Manager
 



**Compensation:** 
 AED 25,000 - AED 35,000 per month + Up to 10% Performance Bonus
 



**Total Annual Package:** 
 Up to AED 480,200 (Tax-Free)
 



**The Reality:** 



* Dubai = 0% personal income tax. Your offer is your take-home.
* In most major markets, 30-50% goes to taxes. Here, 100% of what we offer is what you keep.



**Location:** 
 Dubai, UAE (On-site) with relocation assistance and visa support
 



  





**Role Overview** 




 We're seeking an innovative AI-First UI/UX Design Manager to join our dynamic team at the forefront of internal innovation.
 



  





 You'll report directly to the CEO and hold ultimate ownership of our e-commerce web design, revolutionizing our design process and customer experience through the integration of artificial intelligence while transforming the design team's creative process through AI-driven workflows.
 



  





**The Career Accelerator** 




 You'll compress 3 years of AI-first design leadership mastery into 1. You'll ship high-fidelity web experiences and conversion experiments in your first year. You'll transform how an entire design team works—implementing AI-powered creative operations that eliminate bottlenecks, cutting design to dev handoff time by 40% while rapidly exploring layouts and variants through Figma AI and Firefly, and mentoring your team through a fundamental technological shift that most design leaders won't navigate for years. This pace energises impatient high-performers. It exhausts clock-watchers. If you get frustrated when you're not learning fast enough, you'll thrive here.
 



  





**What Is In It For You:** 




 This isn't just another design leadership role. This is your chance to become a true industry architect.
 



  





**1. Pioneer the Future of AI-Driven Design** 




 You won't just use AI tools—you'll architect an entirely new design operating system. You'll build prompt libraries, AI-assisted workflows, and quality frameworks that eliminate bottlenecks and cut handoff time by 40%. You'll design for conversational shopping, semantic search, and AI assistants while your peers are still debating whether to adopt Figma AI. This is your sandbox to experiment with bandit allocation, LLM-powered recommendations, and Gen AI content operations at scale. You'll master the balance between AI velocity and human craft that most designers won't understand for years.
 



  





**2. Join the Elite Organization and Partner with the 1%:** 



* 🏆 Top 5 Mattress Brand in US with Over
 **$1 Billion USD** 
 in Sales
* 🌍 Great Place to Work Certified
* 💡 Work with Forbes Council Member CEO
* Gain firsthand exposure to strategic decisions and high-velocity execution that drives a global brand



  





**3. Experience a World-Class Lifestyle** 




 Dubai offers a tax-free income that translates to significantly higher take-home pay compared to most global markets. Enjoy a cosmopolitan environment with world-class infrastructure, year-round sunshine, and a thriving international community of 14+ nationalities. This role includes relocation support, premium health coverage, and annual flights home—all while building your career in one of the world's most dynamic business hubs.
 



  





**4. Own the Conversion Engine** 




 You'll have direct P&L impact with clear metrics: add-to-cart conversion lifts, experiment velocity, and design system adoption. You'll present quarterly roadmap reviews directly to the CEO, translating design decisions into business narratives that shape company strategy. This isn't design as decoration—it's design as a revenue driver with full executive visibility and accountability.
 



  





**What You'll Need:** 



* 7–10 years in web design for e-commerce, including 4+ years leading, mentoring, and managing the operations of a design team
* A portfolio that shows shipped PDP/PLP/Checkout improvements tied to clear business metrics
* Expert in Figma (components, variables, Dev Mode); strong visual/motion craft and responsive design sensibility
* Proven experience implementing GenAI tools into a professional design pipeline, with a clear understanding of prompt engineering, quality control, and brand governance
* Familiarity with AI-shaped UX patterns: conversational search, assistant UI, recommendation modules, and their measurement
* Comfort collaborating on experiments (A/B vs multi-armed bandits) with Growth; understands trade-offs between optimization speed and causal certainty
* Working knowledge of WCAG 2.2 and SEO fundamentals for structured content and schema on e-commerce sites



  





**Good-to-Have:** 



* Experience designing chat or guided-selling flows



  





**The Puffy DNA** 




 We're restless, perpetually hungry, and fast-paced, driven by an unwavering belief that we can outperform any competitor — regardless of their size.
 


* **Be the Owner:** 
 We don't hire employees; we welcome owners. You're accountable for results, not activity.
* **Execute with Urgency:** 
 We thrive in high-stakes environments by making thoughtful decisions quickly and acting decisively.
* **Demand Excellence:** 
 We solve the hard problems that others can't, pushing the boundaries of our craft.
* **Be AI-First:** 
 Daily multi-turn AI conversations challenge your assumptions.
* **Go All-In:** 
 When the mission demands it, we rally as one team to cross the finish line.



  





**✨ Your Total Compensation & Benefits** 




  





**💰 Compensation:** 



* Monthly performance bonuses (up to 10%)
* Tax-free income
* Annual return flight allowance to your home country
* Parking allowance (up to AED 7,800 annually)



  





 🏆
 **Rewards & Recognition:** 



* The Leaders Club: Opportunity for selection to an elite lifestyle transforming recognition program featuring premium wellness memberships, exclusive professional networking events, and annual leadership retreats
* 'We Are' Champion: Monthly recognition program with gift vouchers and company-wide celebration for exceptional contributions



  





**🏥 Health & Protection:** 



* Premium worldwide health coverage (AED 1Million limit)
* Life insurance & Personal Accident Insurance (24x monthly salary)



  





**🌴 Time Off:** 



* 22 working days annual leave + UAE public holidays



  





**🌍 Relocation Support (International Hires):** 



* UAE work visa sponsorship
* International flights & airport transfers
* 14-day 4-star hotel accommodation



  





**💼 Work Environment:** 



* AI First organization with access to AI-native tool stack
* Premium office environment in Dubai Internet City
* Learning & development opportunities
* International team collaboration (14+ nationalities)



  





**✅ Other Benefits:** 



* End of Service Gratuity per UAE law



  





**Ready to Shape Your Story?** 




 Click "Apply" and take the first step.

title: Product Analyst - Merchant Information
description:
Visa is a world leader in payments technology, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories, dedicated to uplifting everyone, everywhere by being the best way to pay and be paid.
   

  





 At Visa, you'll have the opportunity to create impact at scale — tackling meaningful challenges, growing your skills and seeing your contributions impact lives around the world. Join Visa and do work that matters — to you, to your community, and to the world.
   

  





 Progress starts with you.
   

  

  

  




**Job Description**

 Tink was founded in 2012, and a decade later became part of Visa in 202. Tink was created with the aim of changing the banking industry for the better. We have built Europe’s most robust open banking platform – with the broadest, deepest connectivity and powerful services that create value out of the financial data. We offer the tools that allow anyone – from big banks and fintech to startups – to build the future of financial services across Europe.
   

  

  

  





 The Data & Risk product family has a clear mission: transforming complex financial data into clear, actionable insights that empower institutions to make smarter decisions and consumers to improve their financial health. We are a tightly knit team of product managers, backend and frontend engineers, platform architects and data scientists working towards enabling data-driven decisions.
   

  

  

  





 What you will do
   

  





 We are looking for a Product Analyst to support the product team working on Merchant Information. You will help translate customer feedback and product needs into clear insights, support day-to-day product execution, and strengthen our quality practices through testing and validation.
   

  





 This role is a great fit for someone who enjoys working with data, product quality, and structured problem solving, and who wants plenty of room to grow into deeper analytical and modelling-oriented work over time.
   

  

 Responsibilities :
   

  




* Support in processing and structuring customer feedback, helping the team identify patterns, common issues, and opportunities to improve product quality and usability
* Support in creating visualizations, querying data, and analyzing quality signals to help track performance and guide product improvements
* Support in testing, validation, and QA, including helping validate behavior changes during migrations and ensuring outputs meet expected standards for accuracy and consistency
* Collaborate closely with product managers, engineers, and other stakeholders to help clarify requirements, document expected behavior, and support reliable delivery across releases
* Help maintain lightweight documentation, reports, and follow-ups that make it easier for teams to understand quality, progress, and where to focus next



 This is a hybrid position. Expectation of days in the office will be confirmed by your Hiring Manager. ​
   

  

  

  




**Qualifications**
* Experience in a product analyst, data analyst, QA, product operations, or similar role in a technology-driven environment
* We are looking for a curious and detail-oriented person who enjoys working with data, product behavior, and quality, and who is comfortable collaborating with technical teams. You don’t need to have done all of the below before — what matters most is strong fundamentals and a willingness to learn.
* Comfort working with data, for example by using SQL, spreadsheets, dashboards, or other tooling to explore issues and communicate insights (tooling can be learned on the job)
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with attention to detail and an interest in understanding how systems behave end-to-end
* Experience supporting testing, validation, or quality assurance activities is a plus
* Clear written and verbal communication skills: you’ll help summarize findings, document outcomes, and support structured collaboration
* Interest in developing deeper expertise over time in areas like quality analysis, structured modelling, and data-driven product improvement


**Additional Information**

 Visa is an EEO Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability or protected veteran status. Visa will also consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with EEOC guidelines and applicable local law.

Games Programming · Stockholm, Sweden
**Midlevel Gameplay Programmer - Stellaris**
============================================


Apply for this job


A galaxy of stories, built by people who care.


We are a passionate team creating a living world that millions of players lose themselves in, and we are looking for a gameplay programmer who wants to help us make it richer with every expansion.

**Your mission**
----------------


As a Gameplay Programmer on Stellaris you will work across the full breadth of the game: implementing new systems for upcoming expansions, improving and refactoring existing features, fixing bugs, and diving into a codebase with nearly a decade of history. You will work shoulder to shoulder with designers and artists to turn their vision into something that actually runs, and you will be expected to contribute opinions and suggestions about what you and the team are building, not just execute tasks. We work closely with our community, which means your work lands in front of real players fast.

**Key responsibilities**
------------------------

* Implement new gameplay systems and features for Stellaris expansions
* Improve, refactor, and maintain existing game systems and keep the codebase healthy
* Build UI and player-facing features across the game
* Investigate and fix bugs, improving the overall player experience
* Collaborate closely with designers, artists and QA contributing your own ideas and feedback throughout the process
* Scope and estimate your own work with a reasonable degree of independence

**Who you are**
---------------


You enjoy learning and are comfortable finding your own way through new challenges. Stellaris has a long development history and the codebase reflects that. There is a lot to discover, and we are looking for someone who finds that engaging.


You know and care about Stellaris. It shapes how you think about the work, and you will have opinions about the game. We want to hear them.


You welcome feedback and stay comfortable when plans shift. Plans change, designs evolve, and sometimes the best decision is to start fresh and you're comfortable with that.


You like working with people: other programmers, designers, artists, the community. You ask questions, you share early, and you are comfortable saying "I think there is a better way to do this."

**What we're looking for**
--------------------------

* Solid C++ knowledge
* Familiarity with Stellaris and a general interest in grand strategy and 4X games
* Someone who can contribute confidently to a design conversation, not just a technical one
* Able to plan and manage your own work, and flag problems early
* Fluent in English, both written and spoken, to effectively collaborate in our international team

**Nice to have**
----------------

* Comfortable with Git
* Visual Studio experience
* Experience modding Stellaris or other Paradox games
* Experience working on a live game with an active player base
* Comfortable planning and tracking work in Jira or a similar tool

**Work culture**
----------------


We are a passionate team that genuinely cares about the games we make and the players who play them. Player feedback shapes everything we do, and we take pride in building something people love.


The team runs on trust. We have a flat hierarchy and high autonomy. You will not always be told exactly how to do your job, but you will be expected to take real ownership of your work. Everyone here has a voice in how the game evolves, and good ideas are welcome regardless of where they come from.


We work collaboratively. Programmers, designers, and artists work closely together, and the best things we make come from all of us building them as a team. If you want to understand the full picture of what you are building and help shape it, you will fit right in.


We believe in growing together. There will be people to learn from, mentors to lean on, and a team that genuinely wants to see you thrive.


If you think that this description matches you, we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible as we will interview candidates continuously. If you don't match all the requirements, please demonstrate how your passion, attitude and interest in Paradox might compensate for this.


At Paradox Interactive we celebrate difference and care deeply about diversity and inclusion. We, therefore, encourage you, no matter who you are, to apply for this job if you think you’d be a great addition to our team.

**Practical information**
-------------------------

* Scope: Full-time and permanent with a 6 -month probation period
* Reports to: Managing Tech Lead
* Location: Stockholm, Sweden. Relocation will not be provided.
* Salary: The salary range for this position starts at SEK 42,000 per month. The final salary will be determined based on the candidate’s relevant experience, qualifications, and overall alignment with the requirements of the role.

  


Department
Games Programming
Role
Game Programmer
Locations
Stockholm, Sweden

Games Programming · Stockholm, Sweden
**Experienced Gameplay Programmer- Stellaris**
==============================================


Apply for this job


A galaxy of stories, built by people who care.


We are a passionate team creating a living world that millions of players lose themselves in, and we are looking for an experienced gameplay programmer who wants to help shape where it goes next.

**Your mission**
----------------


As an Experienced Gameplay Programmer on Stellaris you will be responsible for complex systems across the full breadth of the game: designing and implementing architectural decisions for upcoming expansions, and helping improve the codebase as a whole. You will work shoulder to shoulder with designers and artists, contributing your perspective not just on how things are built but on what gets built and why. You will be someone the team turns to for technical guidance, and someone who helps the people around you grow.

**Key responsibilities**
------------------------

* Design and implement new architectural systems for Stellaris expansions
* Lead technical decisions within your domain and contribute to broader architectural discussions
* Improve, refactor, and maintain existing game systems, with an eye for performance and long-term codebase health
* Investigate and fix bugs, including complex and systemic issues
* Profile and optimise game systems to deliver a smooth player experience
* Collaborate closely with designers, artists and QA bringing both technical depth and design instinct to the conversation
* Help junior and mid-level programmers grow through code review, knowledge sharing, and hands-on guidance
* Plan and scope your own work with a high degree of independence, and flag risks early

**Who you are**
---------------


You are confident and focused. You can navigate a large, evolving codebase, context-switch between tasks without losing momentum, and drive work to completion independently.


You are a great communicator. You share early, explain your thinking clearly, and make it easy for designers, artists, and other programmers to work with you. You are approachable, and people feel comfortable coming to you with questions.


You are a quick learner with a can-do attitude. Stellaris has nearly a decade of development history and there is a lot to discover. You find that energising, not daunting, and you know how to find your footing in unfamiliar territory.


You care about the game, not just the code. You are as interested in the design of what you are building as the technical implementation of it, and you bring opinions and ideas to those conversations.


You are comfortable with change. Plans shift, designs evolve, and sometimes the right call is to start over. You stay grounded and help the team navigate the change.

**What we're looking for**
--------------------------

* 5 or more years of professional C++ experience in game development, including on a live game with an active player base
* Comfortable navigating and working across a large, complex codebase
* Able to own and drive architecture decisions within your domain
* Interest in performance and profiling, and the habits to back it up
* Strong communication skills and the ability to contribute confidently to design conversations
* Comfortable with Git and Visual Studio
* Good written and spoken English

**Nice to have**
----------------

* Familiarity with Stellaris or a general interest in grand strategy and 4X games
* Experience working on games targeting Linux or Mac
* Comfortable planning and tracking work in Jira or a similar tool

**Work culture**
----------------


We are a passionate team that genuinely cares about the games we make and the players who play them. Player feedback shapes everything we do, and we take pride in building something people love.


The team runs on trust. We have a flat hierarchy and high autonomy. You will have real autonomy over how you approach your work, and everyone here has a voice in how the game evolves. Good ideas are welcome regardless of where they come from.


We work collaboratively. Programmers, designers, and artists work closely together, and the best things we make come from all of us building them as a team. Experienced programmers here are a key part of that culture: they raise the level of the whole team, not just their own work.


We believe in growing together. There will be people to learn from, and you will have the chance to be that for others.


If you think that this description matches you, we highly encourage you to apply as soon as possible as we will interview candidates continuously. If you don't match all the requirements, please demonstrate how your passion, attitude and interest in Paradox might compensate for this.


At Paradox Interactive we celebrate difference and care deeply about diversity and inclusion. We, therefore, encourage you, no matter who you are, to apply for this job if you think you’d be a great addition to our team.

**Practical information**
-------------------------

* Scope: Full-time and permanent with a 6 -month probation period
* Reports to: Managing Tech Lead
* Location: Stockholm, Sweden. Relocation will not be provided.
* Salary: The salary range for this position starts at SEK 47,000 per month. The final salary will be determined based on the candidate’s relevant experience, qualifications, and overall alignment with the requirements of the role.

title: Business Analyst - 12 Months Maternity Cover
description:
Business Analyst, GPHE Digital Services ( Maternity Cover)  

Every day, we get opportunities to make a positive impact – on our colleagues, partners, customers, and society. Together, we’re pioneering the solutions of the future and unlocking the full potential of precious resources. Trusted to act on initiative, we challenge conventional thinking to develop world-leading technologies that inspire progress in vital areas, including energy, food, water, and shipping. As we push forward, the innovative, open spirit that fuels our 140-year-old start-up culture and rapid growth also drives our personal growth. So, as we shape a more resourceful, less wasteful world, we build our careers too.
About the role  

At GPHE Digital Service, our mission is to drive innovation and empower the Energy Division at Alfa Laval by enabling cutting-edge digital solutions that boost service sales and streamline service execution. We work hand-in-hand with Alfa Laval’s IT organization, serving as the crucial bridge between technology and business. If you’re passionate about shaping the digital future of service and making a real impact, we’d love to have you on our team!  

As a Business Analyst in the GPHE Digital Service team, you will support the business in making informed, data-driven decisions. Your responsibilities include analyzing data, identifying trends, and presenting actionable insights to stakeholders. By translating complex information into clear recommendations, you will contribute to the development of effective digital solutions for the Business Unit GPHE at Alfa Laval.  

  

Key responsibilities* Turning business needs into clear technical requirements for development.
* Creating and maintaining a list of practical ways analytics can support business goals.
* Analyzing data, building reports or ad hoc analyses (using Power BI), and tracking how analytics tools are used.
* Combining information from different data sources on Alfa Laval’s global data platform to show how data can be used to build new capabilities, establish new ways of working and support business growth.
* Working with business experts to identify and fix data quality issues and making improvements together with different teams.
* Using data to find service sales leads and opportunities and improving how marketing automations are run at Alfa Laval.
* Sharing knowledge and collaborating with analytics teams in other parts of the company.
* Design and deliver training to colleagues across marketing & sales organizations.

  

What you know  

You have been working on data analysis and visualization (preferably Power BI). You also need basic understanding of data infrastructure e.g. table structures and databases like SQL and can contribute to discussions around how to extract and connect data. You have basic project management skills and have experience of designing and delivering training courses in an engaging way. Some experience from a data-driven commercial role in marketing and sales is considered a plus. You have a graduate degree in business administration, information technology, engineering or equivalent. Fluent written and spoken English is a requirement.
Who you are
You genuinely care about your stakeholders and always strive to deliver insights that make a real difference. You’re organized, quality-driven, and approach every challenge with a can-do mindset. You’re passionate about the power of data to drive digital transformation, and you thrive on connecting the big picture with details to ensure priorities are met. Integrity is at the core of who you are, and you value building a positive, collaborative team spirit. This enables you to form strong, trusting relationships across the organization, helping you drive meaningful changes in a complex, matrix environment. You feel right at home working with large datasets and intricate system landscapes, and you enjoy leveraging your data expertise to create impactful solutions.
Assessment practicalities
We care about diversity, inclusion, and equity in our recruitment processes. We also believe behavioral traits can provide important insights into a candidate's fit to a role. To help us achieve this, we apply Pymetrics assessments, and upon application you will be invited to play the assessment games.
For more information, please contact:
Linda Askvid, Senior Project Manager, Linda.Askvid@alfalaval.com
Tanja Sukaj, Talent Acquisition Partner, tanja.sukaj@alfalaval.com
For union information, please contact:
Monica Anderberg, Unionen, at Monica.Anderberg@alfalaval.com
Johan Ranhög, Akademikerna at Johan.Ranhög@alfalaval.com
Stefan Sandell, Ledarna at Stefan.Sandell@alfalaval.com
We review applications continually, so please send yours no later than 25th of March 2026. If the right candidate appears, the ad will be closed earlier. Applications sent directly via email will be disregarded without notice due to General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)

title: Head of Production Management – IBM Platforms
description:
Vill du leda, modernisera och forma framtidens affärskritiska IT-leverans?  

Nu söker vi en erfaren ledare till Production Management med ansvar för våra IBM i- och IBM Z-plattformar – hjärtat i flera av våra mest affärskritiska leveranser.  

  

Det här är en roll för dig som vill kombinera teknik, affär och ledarskap. Du får mandat att utveckla både människor, tjänster och leveransmodeller i en miljö där stabilitet och innovation går hand i hand.

**Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter**


Som Head of Production Management – IBM Platforms har du helhetsansvar för ledning, utveckling och leverans inom våra IBM-baserade plattformstjänster.  

  

Du leder ett chefsled och ett team om specialister och ansvarar för budget, leverans och strategisk utveckling.  

  

**Ditt uppdrag är att:**  

  

. Säkerställa en stabil, säker och konkurrenskraftig IT-leverans  

. Driva modernisering och vidareutveckling av tjänsteutbudet  

. Bygga en framtidssäker leveransmodell inom Managed Services  

. Utveckla kompetens och ledarskap i organisationen  

. Skapa långsiktigt affärsvärde genom effektiv drift och kontinuerlig förbättring  

  

Du verkar i gränslandet mellan strategi och operativ verklighet – där beslut får direkt påverkan på affär och kund.  

  

**Ditt ansvar**  

  

. Övergripande ansvar för Managed Services inom IT-drift och IBM-plattformar  

. Budget-, resultat- och personalansvar  

. Utveckling av kapacitetstjänster, nätverk, backup, storage och övervakning  

. Säkerställande av SLA-efterlevnad och leveranskvalitet  

. Nära samarbete med interna och externa intressenter

**Kvalifikationer för att bli framgångsrik denna roll**

**Vem är du?**  

  

Du är en trygg och tydlig ledare med stark genomförandekraft. Du har erfarenhet av att leda i komplexa IT-miljöer och förstår hur man balanserar stabil drift med förändring och utveckling.  

  

**Vi ser att du har:**  

  

. Flerårig erfarenhet i ledande roller inom IT-infrastruktur och tjänsteleverans  

. Erfarenhet av budget, ekonomistyrning och leveransansvar  

. Förmåga att skapa struktur och tydlighet i dynamiska miljöer  

. Stark kommunikativ förmåga på svenska och engelska  

. Akademisk examen inom IT, teknik eller motsvarande  

  

#LI-HL6  

  

Det är meriterande med erfarenhet från större IT-organisationer eller Managed Services-verksamhet.  

  

**Varför denna roll?**  

  

. En nyckelroll i en affärskritisk organisation  

. Mandat att påverka strategi, modernisering och leveransmodell  

. Möjlighet att utveckla både plattform och människor  

. En ledningsmiljö där ansvar, samarbete och långsiktighet står i centrum  

  

Vill du vara med och vidareutveckla våra mest affärskritiska plattformar och samtidigt ta nästa steg i ditt ledarskap?  

  

Vi ser fram emot din ansökan.  

  

Frågor om tjänsten besvaras av Babak Tabari, babak.tabari@cgi.com  

  

Inför anställning kommer ett utdrag från belastningsregistret och en UC-kontroll att göras.
  

  

**Information om våra förmåner:**  

  

. Aktieprogram och delägarskap: Som delägare har du möjlighet att påverka och i vårt aktieprogram matchar CGI ditt bidrag, krona för krona upp till 3% av din månadslön.  

. Friskvårdsbidrag: Vi erbjuder ett årligt friskvårdsbidrag på 4 000 kr.  

. Föräldralön: Vi betalar upp till cirka 90% av din lön under den första tiden hemma.  

. Trygg anställning: Våra medlemmar anställs med kollektivavtal och försäkringar.  

. Pensionslösningar: Vi erbjuder attraktiva och förmånliga pensionslösningar.  

. Personalbil: Personalbilen används som en privatbil men leasas av CGI.  

  



**Låt oss som ägare omvandla verklig insikt till handling – tillsammans.**  

  

Livet på CGI bygger på ägande, lagarbete, respekt och tillhörighet. Tillsammans med oss kommer du att kunna nå din fulla potential.  

  

Du är inbjuden att ta ägandeskap redan från första dagen i vårt arbete med att förverkliga vår dröm om att skapa en arbetsmiljö där vi trivs med att arbeta tillsammans och bidrar till framgången för vårt företag. Det är därför vi kallar oss CGI Partners snarare än anställda. Vi drar nytta av vår kollektiva framgång och tillsammans får vi möjlighet att aktivt forma vår företagsstrategi och inriktning.  

  

Ditt arbete skapar värde. Du kommer att vara med att utveckla innovativa lösningar och bygga relationer med medarbetare och kunder samtidigt som du får tillgång till globala möjligheter att skala upp dina idéer, omfamna nya möjligheter och dra nytta av gedigen bransch- och teknikexpertis.  

  

Du kommer att forma din karriär tillsammans med ett företag som är byggt för att växa och bestå. Du får stöd av ledare som bryr sig om din hälsa och ditt välbefinnande och ger dig möjligheter att fördjupa dina kunskaper och vidga dina vyer.
  

  

Bli en del en av världens största leverantörer av IT- och affärskonsulttjänster.

title: Head of System Operations, Global Technology Operations,
description:
Vill du vara med och driva hjärtat av vår IT-leverans – där strategi, ekonomi och daglig drift möts? På Global Technology Operations (GTO) söker vi nu en ledare som med tydligt ledarskap och stark genomförandekraft kan ta vår redan satta riktning vidare och förverkliga den i praktiken.  

  

Det här är en roll för dig som trivs med att leda i komplexa miljöer, som vill bidra till en kultur där förbättring, samarbete och ansvar är självklara delar av vardagen – och som ser teknik som en möjliggörare för affär och människor.  

  

Du kommer att leda ett chefsled med direktrapporterande ledare och ha ett indirekt ansvar för en större leveransorganisation. Rollen ingår i en ledningsgrupp som har fullt ansvar för budget, personal och leverans.  

  

**Placering: Stockholm eller Göteborg**

**Dina framtida arbetsuppgifter**


. Säkerställa att strategiska mål omsätts i en stabil, effektiv och affärsdriven IT-leverans  

. Leda och utveckla både människor och tjänsteområden inom operations och infrastruktur  

. Arbeta nära interna och externa intressenter i en komplex leveransmiljö  

. Bidra till en kultur där förbättring, ansvarstagande och samarbete är centralt  

. Ansvara för budget, forecasting och kostnadsstyrning med fokus på affärsvärde  

. Driva och vidareutveckla leveransmodeller inom Managed Services

Ca 8–10 års arbetslivserfarenhet inom IT eller tekniknära verksamhet, gärna med fokus på drift, infrastruktur eller tjänsteleverans  

  

Ca 5 års erfarenhet av formellt personalansvar i komplexa organisationer  

  

Akademisk examen inom IT, teknik eller motsvarande  

  

Dokumenterad erfarenhet av budgetarbete, kostnadsstyrning och ekonomisk uppföljning  

Vi ser gärna att du har erfarenhet av att driva och utveckla leveranser inom Managed Services, gärna i större eller globala organisationer

. Möjlighet att ta nästa steg i din ledarkarriär  

. Ett sammanhang där du får påverka, förbättra och bygga långsiktigt  

. Ett sammansvetsat ledningsteam med högt engagemang och tydligt ansvar  

. En roll där teknik, affär och människor möts – varje dag  

  

#LI-HL6  

  

Vi tror på inkluderande ledarskap, på att olika perspektiv stärker vår leverans, och på att tydlighet och tillit är grunden för framgång. Vi välkomnar särskilt dig som vill bidra med nya tankesätt och som ser värdet i att bygga relationer – både inom och utanför organisationen  

  

Vill du vara med och förverkliga GTO:s strategiska riktning och samtidigt utveckla framtidens IT-leverans? Vi ser fram emot att höra från dig. Frågor om tjänsten besvaras av Babak Tabari, babak.tabari@cgi.com

title: IT/OT specalist
description:
**Description**


**Möjligheten**

**Vi söker en engagerad IT/OT‑specialist som vill spela en nyckelroll i gränssnittet mellan informationssystem (IT) och produktionsteknik (OT). I rollen arbetar du nära både produktion, underhåll och centrala IT‑funktioner för att säkerställa en stabil, säker och framtidssäkrad drift av våra industriella system.**
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------


Tjänsten är både operativ och utvecklingsinriktad, med fokus på hög tillgänglighet, cybersäkerhet och kontinuerlig förbättring. Du kommer att vara en central del i att driva tekniken och våra processer framåt.

 **Hur du kommer att göra skillnad**

* Ansvara för drift, övervakning och vidareutveckling av OT‑miljöer såsom PLC, SCADA, HMI och industriella nätverk
* Säkerställa trygg och effektiv integration mellan IT‑ och OT‑system
* Arbeta med IT/OT‑cybersäkerhet, inklusive segmentering, brandväggar, patchning och behörighetsstyrning
* Leda eller delta i tekniska utredningar samt förbättrings‑ och investeringsprojekt
* Utföra felsökning och hantera incidenter i produktionskritiska system
* Samarbeta nära leverantörer, IT‑avdelning och verksamhetens olika funktioner
* Upprätta och underhålla dokumentation, standarder och riskanalyser

**Din bakgrund**

* Kunskap om virtuella miljöer och backup‑lösningar, vm-ware/lagring net App
* Erfarenhet av virtuella miljöer och backuplösningar, inklusive VMware och NetApp‑lagring
* Eftergymnasial utbildning inom IT, automation, industriell teknik eller motsvarande arbetslivserfarenhet
* Bakgrund inom industriell OT‑miljö samt god förståelse för nätverk, servrar och systemintegration
* Kunskap om industriella styrsystem såsom Siemens, ABB eller Mitsubishi
* Erfarenhet av IT‑säkerhet, gärna med fokus på OT/industri, samt kännedom om ISA/IEC 62443 eller NIS2
* Erfarenhet av produktionssystem (t.ex. MES, historiksystem) samt projektledning eller tekniskt ansvar i industriprojekt
* God kommunikativ förmåga och förmåga att översätta teknik till verksamhetsnytta
* Analytisk, strukturerad, ansvarstagande, nyfiken och lösningsorienterad, med förmåga att arbeta både självständigt och i team

**Vad vi erbjuder**

* Kollektivavtal
* Flexibla arbetstider
* Hälsovård och friskvårdsbidrag
* Fantastiska karriärmöjligheter inom Hitachi Energy, både i Sverige och globalt
* Mentor som stöttar dig under hela introduktionsfasen
* Olika utbildningar och träningsprogram som stödjer medarbetarutveckling
* En diversifierad arbetsplats med över 70 nationaliteter representerade i Sverige
* Kompletterande ersättning vid föräldraledighet
* Förmånsportal med tusentals rabatter och erbjudanden

 **Mer om oss**


Är du redo för en spännande ny utmaning? Låter beskrivningen ovan som dig? Då är du varmt välkommen att söka! Ansökningar behandlas löpande, så vänta inte – skicka in din ansökan redan idag!


Den här tjänsten är placerad i Figeholm  

  

Rekryterandechef: Robert Kallman. robert.kallman@hitachienergy.com kommer att besvara dina frågor om tjänsten. Fackliga representanter – Sveriges Ingenjörer: Katarina Skoglycka, +46 (72) 2265287; Ledarna: Frank Hollstedt, +46 10 738 70 43; Unionen: Jens Lindbäck, +46 (10) 7381868. title: Lead Solution Architect PO2C
description:
**Description**


**Opportunity**

  

We are looking for a Lead Solution Architect for PO2C. In this global role, you will collaborate closely with the Solution Architects, Business Unit Leads (BLs), Archetype Leaders (ALs), and other stakeholders to drive alignment and maximize the use of the SAP functionalities.


You will play a key role in the coordination of the team, continuing the shaping the future of our ERP landscape by embedding the best practices and facilitating the coordination of the key design decisions across business units. This role is as part of Digital Core CoE (Center of Expertise).

**How you will make an impact**

* Implement, develop, and support SAP PS module on SAP S/4HANA.
* Collaborate with Product Owner to define, analyze, and document detailed business requirements.
* Participate in Template Control Board and present new Change Requests for approval.
* Ensure alignment between S/4HANA project design and CR plans; resolve conflicts proactively.
* Map business requirements, provide solutions, prepare functional specifications, and configure the system accordingly.
* Lead implementation of new functionalities and enhancements; conduct Unit, Integration, and Regression Testing; coordinate User Acceptance Testing.
* Oversee vendor execution and delivery, ensuring quality, schedule, and budget compliance; drive vendor teams to meet SLAs and productivity targets.
* Coordinate knowledge transfer with central SAP Product team; prepare documentation for “How To,” Request Fulfilment, and Standard Changes.
* Support Solution Architects in designing training programs and creating training materials and e-learning tools for Key Users.
* Stay updated on industry best practices, participate in process assessments, and suggest innovations to improve processes and functionalities.

**Your background**

* Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business Administration, or equivalent.
* Minimum 8+ years of hands-on experience in SAP PS module, including integration with FI, CO, MM, SD, and PP.
* Proven leadership experience and ability to influence and negotiate across diverse stakeholder groups.
* Strong understanding of Hitachi Energy business processes and cross-functional practices.
* Excellent decision-making, issue-resolution, and strategic thinking skills with a passion for innovation.
* Experience representing business interests in IT projects and driving process improvements.
* Fluent in English with exceptional interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, agile environment and collaborate across all organizational levels.
* Highly collaborative work style and commitment to continuous improvement.

**More about us**


We pride ourselves on offering a holistic range of competitive benefits to support your financial, physical and mental wellbeing and personal development. We want you to truly thrive with us, in work and out.

  

We can provide more information during the recruitment process.  

  

Are you ready for an exciting new challenge? Does the above description sound like you? Applications will be reviewed on an ongoing basis, so don’t delay – apply today!


Recruiting Manager Alexandre Moraes, alexandre.moraes@hitachienergy.com, will answer your questions about the position.
